title: HTML5, CSS3, & Responsive Web Design
description:
Objective

Master modern semantic HTML5 structures, advanced CSS layout techniques (Flexbox and Grid), and responsive design principles for multi-device compatibility.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Semantic HTML5 & Accessibility (a11y)
  • Advanced CSS Flexbox & Grid Layouts
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Media queries and mobile-first workflow
  • CSS custom properties (variables) and pseudo-classes
Learning Resources:
  • MDN Web Docs - CSS – https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Responsive Landing Page

Build a pixel-perfect, fully responsive landing page using semantic HTML5 and modern CSS Grid/Flexbox with mobile-first styling.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Source code directory containing well-organized HTML and CSS files.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain hands-on proficiency in structuring clean web layouts that adapt seamlessly across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.

title: Modern JavaScript (ES6+) & DOM Manipulation
description:
Objective

Understand asynchronous JavaScript, ES6+ features, and interactive DOM manipulation to build dynamic client-side web behavior.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • ES6+ Features (Arrow Functions, Destructuring, Spread/Rest)
  • DOM Manipulation & Event Handling
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Asynchronous JavaScript (Promises, async/await, Fetch API)
  • Event bubbling, delegation, and local storage management
Learning Resources:
  • JavaScript.info – https://javascript.info/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Interactive Web Application (e.g., Weather or Task Tracker)

Build an interactive application that fetches live data from an external public REST API and updates the DOM dynamically based on user input.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • JavaScript modules implementation handling API requests and DOM elements.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Master modern scripting practices and asynchronous data handling required for interactive frontend architectures.

title: React.js Fundamentals & Component Architecture
description:
Objective

Learn React fundamentals, component-driven development, state management hooks, and props flow to build reusable user interfaces.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • JSX, Components, and Props
  • React Hooks (useState, useEffect)
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Component lifecycle and side-effect management
  • Lifting state up and handling user form inputs
Learning Resources:
  • React Official Documentation – https://react.dev/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: React Dashboard or E-Commerce Catalog

Create a modular React application featuring multiple components, dynamic lists, search filters, and reactive state tracking.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Modular React application build folder structure.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain professional expertise in building scalable component hierarchies and managing application state efficiently using React.

title: Routing, State Management & Production Deployment
description:
Objective

Implement multi-page navigation using React Router, manage complex global states, and deploy production-ready applications to Vercel or Netlify.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • React Router & Single Page Application Navigation
  • Production Build Optimization & Deployment
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Dynamic routing, URL parameters, and protected views
  • Deploying static and dynamic apps to Vercel or Netlify
Learning Resources:
  • React Router Documentation – https://reactrouter.com/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Capstone Multi-Page React App

Develop a multi-page single-page application with navigation links, external API calls, and publish the live production build.

Deliverables

  • Live Demo URL (Vercel / Netlify) & Public GitHub Repository URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Fully functional and deployed web application link.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Successfully master client-side routing and cloud deployment pipelines to launch robust frontend web projects live.
